methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

private.name: ClearSpace

data.narrative:
GalaxEye is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Suyash Singh and Kishan Thakkar. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage companies in the same sector, including ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Aalyria, Stoke Space, and Sift.

Two quantitative methods triangulate to a defensible range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to GalaxEye's $10M total raised, implying $20M post-Series A valuation. Peer-median anchoring across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $400M median valuation, with observed range of $274M (Sift, Series B) to $1.3B (Aalyria, Series B). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 45.3x across 6 tickers) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures. Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $17M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $210M.

Key uncertainties center on stage-progression sensitivity—the 2.0x multiplier assumes typical capital efficiency and market conditions—and on peer heterogeneity within Space & Aerospace, where valuations span nearly 5x ($274M to $1.3B) at comparable stages. Without revenue disclosure, P/S anchoring remains unavailable. The $210M median reflects material ambiguity between early-stage capital-proxy methods and peer benchmarking.
